sentences.text: Du har mødt en kvinde
sentences.translation: You met a woman

sentences.text: Det var før, du mødte mig
sentences.translation: It was before you met me

sentences.text: Og for to timer siden rendte I to på hinanden igen
sentences.translation: And two hours ago the two of you ran into each other again

sentences.text: Og det har skræmt dig
sentences.translation: And it has frightened you

sentences.text: Hun var sammen med sin nye kærest'
sentences.translation: She was with her new boyfriend

sentences.text: Som ogs' ligner dig
sentences.translation: Who also looks like you

sentences.text: De holdt i hånden, og hun spurgt', hvordan du har det for tiden
sentences.translation: They held hands, and she asked how you've been lately

sentences.text: Og nu' du ligbleg
sentences.translation: And now you're deathly pale

sentences.text: For hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til
sentences.translation: Because what could life not have become

sentences.text: Hvis det var hende, du var gået med?
sentences.translation: If she had been the one you'd stayed with?

sentences.text: Hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: How long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

sentences.text: Du si'r det til mig midt i dagligstuen
sentences.translation: You tell me in the middle of the living room

sentences.text: Et godt drama her i aftensolen
sentences.translation: A good drama here in the evening sun

sentences.text: Jeg sidder stift i min lænestol
sentences.translation: I sit stiffly in my armchair

sentences.text: Og forsøger at bevare roen
sentences.translation: And try to keep calm

sentences.text: Hun var sammen med sin nye kærest'
sentences.translation: She was with her new boyfriend

sentences.text: Som ogs' ligner dig, det har du sagt
sentences.translation: Who also looks like you, you've said that

sentences.text: Jeg googler hendes navn, det sku' jeg ikk' ha' gjort
sentences.translation: I google her name, I shouldn't have done that

words.definition: googles (borrowed English verb, fully conjugated in Danish)

sentences.text: Nu er jeg helt ødelagt
sentences.translation: Now I'm completely devastated

sentences.text: For hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til
sentences.translation: Because what could life not have become

sentences.text: Hvis det var hende, du var gået med?
sentences.translation: If she had been the one you'd stayed with?

sentences.text: Hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: How long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

sentences.text: Ja, hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til
sentences.translation: Yes, what could life not have become

sentences.text: Hvis det var hende, du var gået med?
sentences.translation: If she had been the one you'd stayed with?

sentences.text: Sig mig, hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: Tell me, how long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

sentences.text: Du har mødt en kvinde
sentences.translation: You met a woman

sentences.text: Hva' ska' jeg stille op?
sentences.translation: What am I supposed to do?

sentences.text: Jeg ka' kun tilbyde den her krop
sentences.translation: I can only offer this body

sentences.text: Er den tilstrækkelig?
sentences.translation: Is it enough?

sentences.text: For hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til?
sentences.translation: Because what could life not have become?

sentences.text: Hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: How long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

sentences.text: Sig mig, hvor længe har du tænkt dig at gå med hendes halskæde?
sentences.translation: Tell me, how long are you planning to keep wearing her necklace?

grammarPoints.rule: Conditional perfect: 'ku' være blevet til' (could have become)
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hva' ku' livet ikk' være blevet til?
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Ku'' = 'kunne', 'være blevet til' = past infinitive of 'blive til'. Together: 'could have become'. Full form: 'Hvad kunne livet ikke være blevet til?' = 'What could life not have become?' This expresses regret about paths not taken.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Gå med nogen' — to date / be in a relationship
grammarPoints.exampleSentence: Hvis det var hende, du var gået med?
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Gå med nogen' is a Danish idiom for being in a romantic relationship. 'Var gået med' = past perfect 'had gone out with'. 'Hvis det var hende, du var gået med' = 'If she had been the one you were with'.

grammarPoints.rule: 'Stille op' — to do / manage / offer a solution
grammarPoints.explanation: 'Stille op' here means 'what can I do?' — expressing helplessness. Very common: 'jeg ved ikke hvad jeg skal stille op' = 'I don't know what to do'. Context changes its meaning (can also mean to set something up or to compete in an election).
